records.ety: [Icel. þeir they, properly nom. pl. masc. of sā, sū, þat, a demonstrative pronoun, akin to the English definite article, AS. sē, se, ðaet, nom. pl. ðā. See That.]

defs: The plural of he, she, or it. They is never used adjectively, but always as a pronoun proper, and sometimes refers to persons without an antecedent expressed.
quotes: Jolif and glad they went unto here [their] rest And casten hem [them] full early for to sail.
Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness.
notes: ☞ They is used indefinitely, as our ancestors used man, and as the French use on; as, they say (French on dit), that is, it is said by persons not specified.
